asserts.note: handling-targets v1 Hatch Handbrake J-turn 180 deg time (FWD front-pull is slow to rotate)
pass shape: yaw settles, no spin past 220 deg (=40 deg past 180)
handling-targets v1 Catchability: yaw under 15 deg/s within 1.2 s, never past 220 deg

KNOWN GRIP-LIMIT RED, re-anchor pending (battery-honest-reds 2026-07-15). The v1 1.4-2.0 s 'crisp RWD rear steps out cleanly' band is an aspiration the tuned grippy tires cannot meet: the coupe J-turn is floored at ~3.50 s (measured, bit-identical x2), same root cause across ALL RWD cars (pickup 4.18 s, kart 4.02 s) -- the accepted grip lift resists a scripted handbrake power-slide, so rotation is slow. Entry-speed and throttle sweeps confirm ~3.5 s is the floor (higher entry = SLOWER). Left red + documented per the standing rule that a real accepted-physics characteristic stays red; re-anchoring the crisp-RWD CHARACTER claim to measured grippy reality is a feel call. See baseline-metrics.md.
pass shape: yaw settles, no spin past 220 deg (measured 5.8 deg -- clean, well inside)

FIXED (battery-honest-reds 2026-07-15): was FALSE (jturnTimeS=0, no 180 deg). The default 0.35 s handbrake initiation was too short to break the coupe's grippy rear loose, so it never rotated -- a PILOT deficiency, not a car limit. hbInitiateS 0.35->1.6 (same lever the pickup already uses at 1.0) breaks the rear loose; the coupe now completes a clean catchable 180 deg (yaw settles < 20 deg/s within 1.5 s, never past 220 deg). NO CarDefinition dial touched.
handling-targets v1 Kart Handbrake J-turn 180 deg time (light RWD rotates almost instantly; stability assist bounds it)
pass shape: yaw settles, no spin past 220 deg
handling-targets v1 Catchability: yaw under 25 deg/s within 1.0 s, never past 220 deg
